description: Fix the uncontested battle popup to show proper force information for allied wins. When a battle is uncontested because two allies share a province, the popup should show each player's force total and indicate who wins the province token. Update the BattlePanel uncontested popup to display force data for allied-win scenarios.
steps: 1. In src/components/BattlePanel.tsx: In the uncontested battle popup section (where battle.uncontested is true), enhance the display. If there are 2 participants and a winner, show both players' forces and indicate who won based on greater force.
2. Import calculateForce from '../utils/gameLogic' if not already imported (it is already imported).
3. For the uncontested popup, show: province name, all participant clan shields and names with their force values, who won (with clan seal), and that they get the war token.
4. For the empty province case (no winner), show that the token is discarded.
5. For single-player uncontested (1 participant), show the player wins without opposition.
acceptance_criteria: Uncontested battle popup for allied provinces shows both players' forces
Uncontested battle popup clearly shows the winner with clan seal
Uncontested battle popup for empty provinces shows token is discarded
Uncontested battle popup for single player shows win without opposition
npm run build succeeds (no new errors)
findings: Enhanced the uncontested battle popup with 3 scenarios: allied (shows both forces), single player (wins without opposition), empty province (token discarded). Also fixed all pre-existing TypeScript errors in BattlePanel.tsx (BattleResultPopup t parameter typed as string instead of TranslationKey) and BattleBiddingOverlay.tsx (template literal as const not assignable to TranslationKey). Build now succeeds with zero TS errors.
